# Feature: SSE Service

## 1. Feature Goal

**ID**: FEAT-005
**Source Plan**: `.spec-lite/plan_websocket_sse.md`

Implement a server-side `SseService` class in `packages/shared/src/services/` that manages the full lifecycle of Server-Sent Events connections from within the Node.js runtime (VS Code extension host or `packages/server`). The service opens SSE streams via `axios` with `responseType: 'stream'`, parses the SSE wire protocol (`event:`, `data:`, `id:`, `retry:`, comments), routes parsed events and status transitions to caller-supplied callbacks via an event-driven `SseConnectionHandle`, and closes connections cleanly. It supports POST-based SSE endpoints (with request body) and custom headers/auth — capabilities that the browser `EventSource` API does not provide. This is the shared backend primitive that both the VS Code adapter (FEAT-008) and the Web server adapter (FEAT-009) will delegate to.

**Business value**: Without this service, neither adapter can establish SSE connections. All SSE UI features in FEAT-007, FEAT-008, and FEAT-009 are blocked on this service being in place.

---

## 2. Data Model (Granular)

No persistent entities — all SSE state is ephemeral and lives in the Zustand store (FEAT-002). This feature adds only runtime types and a service class.

### New Types in `packages/core/src/types/realtime.ts`

These types are placed in core so they can be referenced by the `IRealtimeAdapter` interface (added in FEAT-008) while also being imported by the shared service. They follow the same structure as the existing `WsConnectionConfig` / `WsConnectionHandle`.

- **`SseConnectionConfig`** *(interface)* — parameters passed into `SseService.connect()`:
  - `id` (`string`, readonly, NOT NULL) — caller-assigned unique connection ID (typically `crypto.randomUUID()` at the adapter layer)
  - `method` (`string`, readonly, NOT NULL) — HTTP method for the SSE request (`GET`, `POST`, etc.)
  - `url` (`string`, readonly, NOT NULL) — fully-qualified SSE URL (`http://` or `https://`)
  - `headers` (`Record<string, string>`, readonly, optional) — additional request headers
  - `params` (`string`, readonly, optional) — pre-serialised query string appended to the URL
  - `body` (`unknown`, readonly, optional) — request body for POST-based SSE endpoints
  - `auth` (`Auth`, readonly, optional) — auth configuration; service resolves to headers using the existing `AuthServiceFactory`

- **`SseConnectionHandle`** *(interface)* — the event-driven handle returned by `SseService.connect()`:
  - `connectionId` (`string`, readonly) — matches `SseConnectionConfig.id`
  - `onEvent(cb: (event: SseEvent) => void): Unsubscribe` — fires for every parsed SSE event
  - `onStatusChange(cb: (status: ConnectionStatus) => void): Unsubscribe` — fires on each lifecycle transition
  - `onError(cb: (error: string) => void): Unsubscribe` — fires on connection or stream errors
  - `onHeaders(cb: (headers: Record<string, string>) => void): Unsubscribe` — fires once with the initial HTTP response headers

### Internal Types in `packages/shared/src/services/SseService.ts`

- **`ActiveSseConnection`** *(internal interface, not exported)* — one entry per open SSE stream kept in the service's private `Map<string, ActiveSseConnection>`:
  - `abortController` — `AbortController` instance for cancelling the axios request
  - `config` — the original `SseConnectionConfig`
  - `status` — cached `ConnectionStatus`
  - `connectedAt?` — unix epoch ms when the connection received the HTTP response
  - `lastEventId?` — the most recently received SSE `id:` field value
  - `retryMs?` — the server-suggested retry interval from `retry:` field (stored but not acted upon — manual reconnect only)
  - `eventListeners` — `Set<(event: SseEvent) => void>`
  - `statusListeners` — `Set<(status: ConnectionStatus) => void>`
  - `errorListeners` — `Set<(error: string) => void>`
  - `headerListeners` — `Set<(headers: Record<string, string>) => void>`

- **`SseFrameParser`** *(internal class or function, not exported)* — stateful line-oriented SSE frame parser:
  - Accumulates lines from a stream chunk (split on `\n` or `\r\n`)
  - Processes field lines: `event:`, `data:`, `id:`, `retry:`, and ignores comment lines (`:`)
  - On encountering a blank line: dispatches the accumulated event (eventName defaults to `'message'` when no `event:` field was present) and resets
  - Handles multi-line `data:` (concatenated with `\n`)
  - Returns parsed frames one-by-one to the caller via a callback

### Relationships

- `SseService` 1:N `ActiveSseConnection` — one service instance manages many connections; each keyed by `connectionId`.
- `SseConnectionHandle` references `ActiveSseConnection` — the handle's `on*` methods add/remove listeners from the internal sets.

### Constraints / Limits

- Maximum 10 concurrent active connections enforced at `connect()` time (same constant `MAX_CONNECTIONS` as `WebSocketService`; connections are tracked independently per service).
- URL scheme must be `http:` or `https:` — all other schemes are rejected before any network I/O.

---

## 4. Dependencies

| Feature | Reason |
|---------|--------|
| FEAT-001 — Protocol Types & Core Data Model | Provides `SseCollectionRequest`, `RequestProtocol`, type guards |
| FEAT-002 — Realtime State Slice | Provides `ConnectionStatus`, `SseEvent`, `RealtimeTabState` already in `packages/core/src/types/realtime.ts`; this feature extends that file |
| `AuthServiceFactory` in `packages/shared` | `setSseAuthServiceFactory` pattern follows the same approach as `WebSocketService.setWsAuthServiceFactory()` for auth header resolution |
| `axios ^1.14` in `packages/shared` | Already installed. Used with `responseType: 'stream'` for the SSE connection |
| `storeService` in `packages/shared` | Provides `getHttpsAgentForUrl()` for TLS/proxy support on `https://` SSE endpoints |

> FEAT-008 (VS Code Adapter) and FEAT-009 (Web Adapter) depend on this feature — they cannot fully integrate SSE until FEAT-005 is complete.
> `IRealtimeAdapter` and its addition to `IPlatformAdapter` are **deferred to FEAT-008**.

## 5. Implementation Tasks

### TASK-001: Add SSE connection types to `@wave-client/core`

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  Extend `packages/core/src/types/realtime.ts` — after the existing `WsConnectionHandle` interface, add:

  1. `SseConnectionConfig` interface with fields: `id` (readonly string), `method` (readonly string), `url` (readonly string), `headers?` (readonly Record), `params?` (readonly string), `body?` (readonly unknown), `auth?` (readonly, imported `Auth` type).
  2. `SseConnectionHandle` interface with fields: `connectionId` (readonly string), `onEvent(cb): Unsubscribe`, `onStatusChange(cb): Unsubscribe`, `onError(cb): Unsubscribe`, `onHeaders(cb): Unsubscribe`.

  Extend `packages/core/src/index.ts`:
  - Add `SseConnectionConfig`, `SseConnectionHandle` to the types re-exported from `./types/realtime`.

- [ ] **Unit Tests**:
  No runtime logic introduced — TypeScript compilation is the verification gate. If the types are consumed in TASK-002's tests without cast errors, they are correct.

  Add JSDoc comments to each new type/interface in `realtime.ts`:
  - `SseConnectionConfig`: describe each field (url scheme requirement for http/https, how params are appended, body usage for POST-based SSE, auth resolution).
  - `SseConnectionHandle`: describe the event-driven model; note that `onEvent` fires for each parsed SSE event (not raw chunks), `onStatusChange` fires `'connecting'` immediately, `onHeaders` fires once with the initial HTTP response headers.

- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core build` exits with code 0; `SseConnectionConfig` and `SseConnectionHandle` appear in `packages/core/dist/index.d.ts`.

### TASK-002: Implement SSE frame parser

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  In `packages/shared/src/services/SseService.ts`, implement the SSE wire protocol parser as an internal (non-exported) component of the service file. The parser should:

  1. Accept incremental text chunks from an axios stream (chunks may contain partial lines, multiple lines, or multiple events).
  2. Maintain a line buffer for incomplete lines across chunks (streams do not guarantee line-aligned delivery).
  3. Split each chunk on `\n` (handling both `\n` and `\r\n` line endings).
  4. For each complete line:
     - Lines starting with `:` → comment, ignore entirely.
     - Lines matching `event:<value>` → set the pending event name (trim leading single space from value per SSE spec).
     - Lines matching `data:<value>` → append to the pending data buffer (multi-line data joined with `\n`).
     - Lines matching `id:<value>` → set the pending event ID. If the value contains a null character (`\0`), ignore the field (per SSE spec).
     - Lines matching `retry:<value>` → if value consists solely of digits, parse as integer and store as the retry interval.
     - Empty line (`""` or `"\r"`) → dispatch the accumulated event:
       - `eventName` defaults to `'message'` if no `event:` field was seen.
       - `data` is the accumulated data buffer (without trailing `\n`).
       - If the data buffer is empty, do NOT dispatch (no-op per SSE spec).
       - Reset the pending event name, data buffer, and event ID after dispatch.
  5. Expose via a callback or method that the main service class calls with each stream chunk.

  Implementation pattern — an internal class `SseFrameParser`:
  ```
  class SseFrameParser {
      private _lineBuffer = '';
      private _eventName = '';
      private _dataBuffer = '';
      private _eventId: string | undefined;
      private _onEvent: (eventName: string, data: string, eventId?: string) => void;
      private _onRetry: (ms: number) => void;

      constructor(
          onEvent: (eventName: string, data: string, eventId?: string) => void,
          onRetry: (ms: number) => void,
      ) { ... }

      /** Feed a raw chunk from the stream. May trigger zero or more event dispatches. */
      processChunk(chunk: string): void { ... }

      /** Flush any remaining data in the buffer (called on stream end). */
      flush(): void { ... }
  }
  ```

- [ ] **Unit Tests** (`packages/shared/src/test/services/SseService.test.ts`):

  | ID | Scenario | Assertion |
  |----|----------|-----------|
  | T01 | Single event in one chunk: `data: hello\n\n` | `onEvent` called once with eventName=`'message'`, data=`'hello'` |
  | T02 | Named event: `event: update\ndata: payload\n\n` | eventName=`'update'`, data=`'payload'` |
  | T03 | Multi-line data: `data: line1\ndata: line2\n\n` | data=`'line1\nline2'` |
  | T04 | Event with ID: `id: 42\ndata: hello\n\n` | eventId=`'42'` |
  | T05 | ID containing null character is ignored | `id:` field with `\0` → eventId is `undefined` |
  | T06 | Retry field: `retry: 3000\n\n` | `onRetry` called with `3000`; no event dispatched (empty data) |
  | T07 | Retry field with non-numeric value is ignored | `retry: abc\n` → `onRetry` not called |
  | T08 | Comment lines are ignored: `: this is a comment\ndata: hello\n\n` | eventName=`'message'`, data=`'hello'` |
  | T09 | Empty data buffer → no dispatch: `event: ping\n\n` | `onEvent` not called |
  | T10 | Partial chunk delivery: `da` + `ta: hello\n\n` across two processChunk calls | Correctly buffered and dispatched |
  | T11 | Multiple events in one chunk: `data: a\n\ndata: b\n\n` | `onEvent` called twice |
  | T12 | `\r\n` line endings handled correctly | Same parsing as `\n` |
  | T13 | Leading space after colon is trimmed: `data:  two spaces\n\n` | data=`' two spaces'` (only first space trimmed per spec) |
  | T14 | Field with no value: `data\n\n` | data is empty string → no dispatch |
  | T15 | `flush()` dispatches pending event if data buffer is non-empty | Feed `data: hello\n` (no trailing blank line), then `flush()` → event dispatched |

- [ ] **Documentation Update**:
  Add JSDoc to `SseFrameParser` class and its `processChunk` / `flush` methods. Document the SSE specification compliance (reference: https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/server-sent-events.html#parsing-an-event-stream). Add inline comments for the line-buffer logic and the empty-dispatch rule.

- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/shared test -- --grep "SseFrameParser"` passes all T01–T15. The parser is tested in isolation before integrating with the service.

### TASK-003: Implement `SseService` — connection lifecycle (connect + disconnect + event routing)

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  In `packages/shared/src/services/SseService.ts`, implement the main service class:

  ```
  Responsibilities:
  - Private `_connections: Map<string, ActiveSseConnection>` — keyed by connectionId.
  - MAX_CONNECTIONS = 10 module-level constant.
  - Injectable auth service factory via `setSseAuthServiceFactory(factory)` — mirrors `setWsAuthServiceFactory` exactly.

  - async connect(config: SseConnectionConfig): Promise<SseConnectionHandle | null>
      1. Validate URL scheme — must be http: or https:. Log warning + return null otherwise.
      2. Check connection limit. Log warning + return null if at max.
      3. Build request headers:
         - Start with config.headers (if any).
         - Add `Accept: text/event-stream` (SSE content negotiation).
         - Add `Cache-Control: no-cache` (prevent caching of the event stream).
         - If config.auth is set and enabled, resolve auth headers via _resolveAuthHeaders().
      4. Append config.params to URL query string if provided.
      5. Retrieve HTTPS agent for https:// via storeService.getHttpsAgentForUrl(url).
      6. Create an AbortController for this connection.
      7. Create the ActiveSseConnection record (status starts as 'connecting').
      8. Store in _connections.
      9. Emit 'connecting' status to listeners.
      10. Fire axios request asynchronously (non-blocking):
          {
              method: config.method,
              url: targetUrl,
              headers: requestHeaders,
              data: config.body,
              responseType: 'stream',
              httpsAgent: agent,
              signal: abortController.signal,
              // No timeout — SSE streams are long-lived
          }
      11. On axios response:
          - Emit 'connected' status.
          - Capture response headers and emit via headerListeners.
          - Set up SseFrameParser.
          - Pipe response.data (Node Readable stream) through:
            - stream.on('data', chunk => parser.processChunk(chunk.toString('utf8')))
            - Parser onEvent callback → create SseEvent, emit to eventListeners, update lastEventId.
            - Parser onRetry callback → update connection retryMs.
          - stream.on('end') → emit 'disconnected' status, remove from _connections.
          - stream.on('error', err) → emit error to errorListeners, emit 'error' status, remove.
      12. On axios error (request rejected, network error, abort) → emit error + 'error' status, remove.
      13. Build and return SseConnectionHandle (closure, same pattern as WebSocketService._buildHandle).

  - async disconnect(connectionId: string): Promise<Result<void, string>>
      1. Look up connection; return err if not found.
      2. Emit 'disconnecting' status.
      3. Call abortController.abort().
      4. Return ok(void). (The stream 'error'/'end' event will clean up.)

  - private _resolveAuthHeaders(auth, baseHeaders) — same logic as WebSocketService: ApiKey/Basic/Bearer supported, Digest skipped with warning.

  - private _emitStatus(connectionId, status) — same pattern as WebSocketService.
  - private _emitHeaders(connectionId, headers) — same pattern as WebSocketService.
  - private _removeConnection(connectionId) — same pattern as WebSocketService.
  - private _buildHandle(connectionId, conn) — same pattern as WebSocketService but with onEvent instead of onMessage.
  ```

  Export a module-level singleton: `export const sseService = new SseService();`

  Update `packages/shared/src/services/index.ts`:
  - Add `export { SseService, sseService, setSseAuthServiceFactory } from './SseService';`

- [ ] **Unit Tests** (append to `packages/shared/src/test/services/SseService.test.ts`):

  Mock axios to return a controllable Readable stream:
  ```
  // Create a PassThrough stream as the mock response data
  const mockStream = new PassThrough();
  vi.mocked(axios).mockResolvedValue({
      status: 200,
      headers: { 'content-type': 'text/event-stream', 'x-request-id': 'abc' },
      data: mockStream,
  });
  ```

  Also mock `storeService` (same pattern as WebSocketService tests).

  | ID | Scenario | Assertion |
  |----|----------|-----------|
  | T16 | Valid `http://` URL — connect succeeds | `axios` called with correct URL, method, and `responseType: 'stream'` |
  | T17 | Valid `https://` URL — HTTPS agent requested | `storeService.getHttpsAgentForUrl` called; agent passed in axios config |
  | T18 | `onStatusChange` fires `connecting` then `connected` | Call connect; verify `connecting` callback; resolve axios; verify `connected` |
  | T19 | `onHeaders` fires with initial response headers | Resolve axios with headers; verify header callback |
  | T20 | `onEvent` fires for parsed SSE events from stream | Push `data: hello\n\n` into mock stream; verify event callback with eventName=`'message'`, data=`'hello'` |
  | T21 | Multiple events in stream are all dispatched | Push two events; verify two callback invocations |
  | T22 | Named SSE event parsed correctly | Push `event: update\ndata: payload\n\n`; verify eventName=`'update'` |
  | T23 | Stream end emits 'disconnected' status | End mock stream; verify `disconnected` via onStatusChange |
  | T24 | Stream error emits error + 'error' status | Emit error on mock stream; verify onError and 'error' status |
  | T25 | `disconnect()` emits 'disconnecting' and aborts request | Call disconnect; verify 'disconnecting' via onStatusChange; verify abortController.abort called |
  | T26 | `disconnect()` returns err for unknown connectionId | `Result.isOk === false` with descriptive message |
  | T27 | Invalid scheme `ws://` — connect returns null | No axios call made |
  | T28 | Invalid scheme `javascript:` — connect returns null | No axios call made |
  | T29 | Connection limit — 11th connect returns null | Open 10 connections first; 11th returns null |
  | T30 | `Unsubscribe` removes listener — no further callbacks | Register `onEvent`, unsubscribe, push event, verify cb not called |
  | T31 | `onStatusChange` fires immediately with current status for late subscribers | Connect + resolve axios; add new onStatusChange; verify immediate `connected` call |
  | T32 | `Accept: text/event-stream` header is always set | Verify axios called with this header regardless of user headers |
  | T33 | `Cache-Control: no-cache` header is always set | Verify axios called with this header |
  | T34 | POST method with body — body passed to axios | Connect with method='POST', body={key:'val'}; verify axios data field |
  | T35 | Query params appended to URL correctly | Connect with params='a=1&b=2'; verify axios URL contains params |
  | T36 | Malformed SSE frame (no data) does not dispatch event | Push `event: ping\n\n`; verify onEvent not called |
  | T37 | Malformed URL — connect returns null | Connect with `'not-a-url'`; returns null |

- [ ] **Documentation Update**:
  Add JSDoc to `SseService` class, `connect()`, `disconnect()`, `MAX_CONNECTIONS`. Document:
  - The axios streaming approach and why `EventSource` is not used (POST support, custom headers).
  - Auth support scope (same as WebSocketService).
  - The abort mechanism for disconnect.
  - Add `SseService` mention to `packages/shared/README.md`.

- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/shared test` passes all new tests (T01–T37) and all pre-existing shared tests. `pnpm --filter @wave-client/shared build` exits 0.

### TASK-004: Auth/proxy integration and SSE-specific headers

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  In `packages/shared/src/services/SseService.ts`:

  **`_resolveAuthHeaders(auth: Auth, baseHeaders: Record<string, string>): Promise<Record<string, string>>`**:
  - Uses the injectable `sseAuthServiceFactory` (set via `setSseAuthServiceFactory()`; defaults to `AuthServiceFactory`).
  - Only handles `API_KEY`, `BASIC`, and `OAUTH2_REFRESH` (Bearer) — `DIGEST` auth emits a `console.warn("Auth type 'digest' is not supported for SSE connections — skipping auth.")` and returns `baseHeaders` unchanged.
  - If `sseAuthServiceFactory` is not set (null), returns `baseHeaders` unchanged.
  - Same error handling as `WebSocketService._resolveAuthHeaders`: on auth failure, log warning and proceed without auth.

  Ensure `connect()` always sets these headers on every SSE request (in addition to user-supplied and auth-resolved headers):
  - `Accept: text/event-stream` — standard SSE content negotiation.
  - `Cache-Control: no-cache` — prevents intermediary caches from buffering the stream.
  - User-supplied headers with the same keys override these defaults (user intent takes precedence).

  **Proxy/TLS support**:
  - For `https://` URLs, call `storeService.getHttpsAgentForUrl(url)` and pass the resulting agent to the axios config's `httpsAgent` field. Same pattern as WebSocketService does for `wss://`.

- [ ] **Unit Tests** (append to `packages/shared/src/test/services/SseService.test.ts`):

  | ID | Scenario | Assertion |
  |----|----------|-----------|
  | T38 | Auth: ApiKey auth adds header to SSE request | Mock authServiceFactory returning header `{ 'X-Api-Key': 'key123' }`; connect; verify axios called with header |
  | T39 | Auth: Bearer auth adds Authorization header | Mock; verify `Authorization: Bearer tok` in axios headers |
  | T40 | Auth: Digest auth is skipped with warning | Mock auth type `digest`; connect; verify no auth header; verify `console.warn` called |
  | T41 | Auth: no authServiceFactory set → headers unchanged | Set factory to null; connect; no error thrown; `Accept` header still present |
  | T42 | User-supplied `Accept` header overrides default | Connect with headers `{ Accept: 'text/html' }`; verify axios called with `Accept: text/html` |
  | T43 | `https://` URL uses HTTPS agent from storeService | Connect with `https://`; verify `storeService.getHttpsAgentForUrl` called; agent in axios config |
  | T44 | `http://` URL does NOT request HTTPS agent | Connect with `http://`; verify `storeService.getHttpsAgentForUrl` not called |

- [ ] **Documentation Update**:
  Update JSDoc for `_resolveAuthHeaders()` and `connect()` to document the auth types supported / skipped. Document the default SSE headers and the override behavior.

- **Verify**: All tests T01–T44 pass. `pnpm --filter @wave-client/shared build` exits 0. `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core build` exits 0.

---

## 6. Cross-Cutting Concerns

- **Auth**: Bearer, API key, and Basic auth are resolved to request headers applied to the initial SSE HTTP request. This mirrors the `WebSocketService` auth pattern. Digest and NTLM are not applicable to SSE (Digest requires challenge-response which is incompatible with streaming) and are silently skipped with a `console.warn`. Auth secrets are never logged.
- **Error Handling**: `connect()` returns `null` (not `Result`) for pre-flight validation failures (invalid URL, limit exceeded) because no handle exists yet — callers must null-check. `disconnect()` returns `Result<void, string>`. Runtime stream errors (network drop, server close, malformed response) are surfaced via the `onError` and `onStatusChange` callbacks on the handle. Malformed SSE frames are logged at `console.warn` level but do not terminate the stream — the parser skips them.
- **Logging**: Log at `console.info` level: connection attempts (`[SseService] connect attempt id=<id> method=<method> url=<url>`), successful stream open (`[SseService] connected id=<id>`), stream end (`[SseService] disconnected id=<id>`), and errors (`[SseService] error id=<id> msg=<message>`). Log SSE parse errors at `console.warn` level with truncated frame content (max 200 chars). Never log auth headers or tokens.
- **Security**: URL scheme validation is enforced synchronously before any auth resolution or network I/O. Connection limits prevent resource exhaustion. TLS certificate settings are applied via the existing `storeService.getHttpsAgentForUrl()` agent. The SSE parser does not use `eval()` or execute any code from event payloads.
- **Performance**: Listener sets use native `Set`; `_emitStatus` iterates the set synchronously on the Node.js event loop. The SSE frame parser is stateful and processes chunks incrementally — it does not buffer entire responses in memory, making it suitable for long-lived streams.

---
